mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an approaching labor market peak and impending earnings recession, causing equity valuations to contract sharply within the next week as investors price in cyclical downturn risks.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during historical labor market peaks.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding consumer credit balances indicate robust aggregate demand growth, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with forward returns across three consecutive business cycles, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which improves profit margins and sustains positive equity returns during the initial phases of economic recovery.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth exceeds the 75th percentile of historical expansion rates, indicating that supply booms no longer translate to equity outperformance.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly correlates with rising corporate earnings per share, establishing a persistent upward trend in equity valuations that compounds over a six-month forward window.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year Spearman correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns drops below 0.10, or negative GDP growth periods consistently yield positive equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When headline inflation consistently outpaces the federal funds rate by more than one percentage point, real interest rates turn negative, triggering asset price inflation and driving strong positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Real rates turn negative but equity returns remain flat or decline, or the spread signal fails to generate a positive Rank IC above 0.05 during high-inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which boosts consumer spending power and drives immediate positive equity returns as earnings revisions turn sharply positive.
failure_condition: The dual-signal combination produces a negative predictive relationship for 5-day returns during three consecutive labor market expansions, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.8 million annualized units signal sustained construction activity and mortgage demand, which historically precedes broad-based economic acceleration and generates positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns turn negative when HOUST remains above 1800 for more than two consecutive quarters, indicating that housing saturation eventually drags on broader economic growth.
